title: Design virtual-memory mappings deliberately
description: Set page protections, mapping lifetimes, and TLB flushes correctly so memory is no more permissive than needed and mappings stay coherent.
body: Use when manipulating page tables, mmap handlers, or kernel mappings. Do not leave pages writable-and-executable, forget a TLB flush after changing a mapping, or map more than the driver needs. Actions: set the least-permissive page protections, flush the TLB/caches after changing mappings, validate user mmap requests against allowed ranges, and tear mappings down on cleanup. Validation signals include correct fault behavior on protected accesses and no stale-TLB corruption under stress. Risk boundary: a missing TLB flush causes silent data corruption and a W+X or over-broad mapping is an exploit primitive; treat mapping-permission and flush errors as blocking and review them.
